mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values during a post-shock recovery phase reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher during recovery phases when INDPRO rises, or the signal flips direction in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 320.0 signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, supporting higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 4-year rolling window or fails to predict positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when housing starts decline in late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.
